mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which mechanically lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25%, ultra-loose monetary policy suppresses real discount rates and induces a reach-for-yield dynamic that lifts equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in the majority of observed instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales contemporaneous with unemployment above 8% captures a demand recovery that outpaces labor market adjustment, generating positive earnings surprises and S&P 500 monthly returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition of positive RSAFS growth and UNRATE above 8% produces negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year window, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8% reflects severe demand deficiency and rising corporate default risk, generating persistent risk-off flows that drive negative S&P 500 weekly returns.
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE exceeds 8%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 600 thousand units signal a deeply depressed construction sector where marginal policy stimulus or household formation recovery generates outsized mean-reversion in housing-sensitive equities, lifting broad S&P 500 quarterly returns.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 600, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered quarters over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing orders and capacity utilization, translating directly into higher expected operating leverage and positive S&P 500 monthly returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Subdued monthly consumer price inflation below 0.15 index points reduces the probability of imminent monetary tightening, lowering the equity risk premium and generating positive S&P 500 weekly returns.
failure_condition: In months where CPIAUCSL 1-month change is below 0.15 index points, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarterly growth in real GDP signals an economy-wide contraction in aggregate demand that depresses top-line corporate revenues and compresses valuation multiples, driving negative S&P 500 quarterly returns.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 quarterly change is negative, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered quarters over a 3-year window.
